title: Palmer Sen. Shelley Hughes joins crowded Republican field for 2026 Alaska governor’s race
summary: Sen. Shelley Hughes, R-Palmer, speaks with Senate Minority Leader Mike Shower, R-Wasilla, during a break in the Alaska Senate on Monday, April 28, 2025. (Photo by James Brooks/Alaska Beacon) Palmer Sen. Shelley Hughes has become the seventh Republican to announce a run for governor in next year’s election. Incumbent Gov. Mike Dunleavy, a Republican, is term-limited and cannot seek reelection in 2026. With an announcement...

title: AST Criminal Trespass 2nd: Mile 188 Parks Hwy [AK25073733]
summary: On 07/25/2025 at 1351 hours AST received a report of multiple individuals trespassing at The Igloo, a building that looks like an igloo at Mile 188 Parks Highway. AST located and contacted the individuals in Cantwell. A misdemeanor summons for Criminal Trespass 2nd was issued to Aero Wilson, age 18....
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Enjoy the upcoming warm weather, but be aware of outdoor closures, fire restrictions and fire safety
summary: Take these steps to make sure your campfire is completely extinguished. As you enjoy the warmer weather this weekend, please remember to practice fire safety. Ensure any outdoor fires are completely extinguished, and never leave them unattended. Start the weekend off right by being mindful of area closures, fire restrictions and keeping a safe distance from dry vegetation. Together, we can hel...

title: US Education Department to unfreeze contested K-12 funds
summary: The Lyndon Baines Johnson Department of Education Building in Washington, D.C., in a file photo from November 2024. (Photo by Shauneen Miranda/States Newsroom) WASHINGTON — The Trump administration said Friday it’ll soon release billions in Education Department funding that has been frozen for weeks, delaying disbursements to K-1...

title: AST DV Criminal Mischief: Palmer [AK25073791]
summary: On 07/25/2025 at approximately 1808 hours, AST was dispatched to a report of a disturbance off of Palmer Fishhook Road. Upon investigation, it was determined that William Parkinson, 49, of Palmer, had broken property and was subsequently charged with Domestic Violence - Criminal Mischief V. William....

title: AST Burglary: North Pole [AK25073837]
summary: On July 25, 2025, at approximately 1756 hours, Fairbanks AST received a report of a burglary in the area of Lineman Avenue resulting in missing coolers, tools, and water tanks. Investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Fatal Vehicle Collision: Wasilla [AK25073602]
summary: On July 25, 2025, Alaska State Troopers were notified around 1 p.m. of a head-on collision near Mile 81 of the Parks Highway. The initial investigation showed a Kia Rio traveling southbound crossed the center line and struck a Honda Civic. Connal M. Dinsmore, 19 of Wasilla, was deceased at the scene...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Governor Dunleavy Announces Revenue Commissioner Adam Crum’s Resignation
summary: Governor Mike Dunleavy today announced the resignation of Adam Crum, Commissioner of the Department of Revenue, effective August 8, 2025. Governor Dunleavy expressed appreciation for Commissioner Crum’s dedicated leadership, professionalism, and contributions during his tenure. “Commissioner Crum ha...

title: Goldstream Creek and Bonanza Creek fires see continued suppression success
summary: Bonanza Creek Fire (#238): 12,566 acres, 15% containment Goldstream Creek Fire (#270): 20,483 acres, 41% containment Personnel: 363 Firefighters hike down the fire line on the Nenana Ridge Complex. July 21, 2025. Daniel Gutierrez / Florida Forest Service Evacuation Information: Evacuation levels were downgraded Monday, July 21st by the Fairbanks North Star Borough and the Alaska Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Management along the Parks Highway corridor. Level 3 ‘GO!’: Outside the Borough: From the borough’s western edge, 4.5 miles west; from Sled Road north for 7 miles from the Alaska Railroad. This...

title: Friday, July 25, 2025
summary: The statewide Turquoise Alert system in Arizona, initially touted as a way to swiftly disseminate info about Indigenous people who’ve gone missing, was enacted earlier this month. But as KJZZ’s Gabriel Pietrorazio reports, the first-ever alert helped safely locate a girl from Hawaii. “We are here because the crisis of missing and murdered Indigenous people demands our full attention an...

title: Alaska school districts join lawsuit over Trump administration freeze of billions for education
summary: Pearl Creek Elementary School is seen on June 3, 2025. The Fairbanks North Star Borough School District decided to close the school at the end of the academic year due to budget cuts. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) Three Alaska school districts have joined a coalition from across the country — including school districts, teachers unions, parents and advocacy groups — suing the Trump administration for blocking $6.8 billion in congressionally approved education funding. The Anchorage, Fairbanks North Star Borough, and the Kuspuk school districts joined the lawsuit filed Monday in federal court. The suit challenges the withholding...

summary: On 7/24/2025 Tok Wildlife Troopers cited Jamie Bailey, age 26, of Anchorage for failing to record fish caught during a personal use fishery. Bail was set at $120 in the Kenai District Court.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Fail to Record Salmon on Personal Use Permit

title: AST Fail to Mark Personal Use Fish - Clip Tails: Kenai North Beach [AK25073432]
incident_type: Fail to Mark Personal Use Fish - Clip Tails
summary: On 7/25/25 Tok Wildlife Troopers cited Tanarius Brown, age 38, of Anchorage for failing to mark/clip the tails of personal use fish as required. Bail was set at $95 in the Kenai District Court.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Fail to Mark Personal Use Fish - Clip Tails

title: AST DUI: Fairbanks [AK25073416]
summary: On July 25, 2025, at approximately 0211 hours, while conducting traffic enforcement, Alaska State Troopers observed a vehicle driving Southbound on University Ave without any of its head/taillights on. Troopers conducted a traffic stop on the vehicle and, following an investigation, found the driver, Kevin Franks (39M), to be impaired by alcohol. Mr. Franks was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center for one count of DUI without further issue.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: University Ave without any of its head/taillights on
